title: The Quran
subtitle: Holy Scripture of Islam
description: The central religious text of Islam, believed to be a revelation from God (Allah) through the Archangel Gabriel to the Prophet Muhammad over approximately 23 years.
summary: The Quran is the literal word of God as revealed to Prophet Muhammad. It covers guidance for personal conduct, legal matters, theological principles, and stories of previous prophets. It emphasizes monotheism (tawhid), justice, compassion, and submission to God's will.

verses.text: In the name of Allah, the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful.
verses.translation: In the name of Allah, the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful.
verses.explanation: This opening chapter of the Quran, Al-Fatiha, is a prayer for guidance, praising God as the Most Gracious, Most Merciful, and Master of the Day of Judgment.

translations.text: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.

verses.text: All praise is due to Allah, Lord of the worlds.
verses.explanation: This opening chapter of the Quran, Al-Fatiha, is a prayer for guidance, praising God as the Most Gracious, Most Merciful, and Master of the Day of Judgment.

translations.text: Praise be to Allah, the Cherisher and Sustainer of the worlds:

verses.text: The Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful.
verses.explanation: This opening chapter of the Quran, Al-Fatiha, is a prayer for guidance, praising God as the Most Gracious, Most Merciful, and Master of the Day of Judgment.

verses.text: Sovereign of the Day of Recompense.
verses.explanation: This opening chapter of the Quran, Al-Fatiha, is a prayer for guidance, praising God as the Most Gracious, Most Merciful, and Master of the Day of Judgment.

translations.text: Master of the Day of Judgment.

verses.text: It is You we worship and You we ask for help.
verses.explanation: This opening chapter of the Quran, Al-Fatiha, is a prayer for guidance, praising God as the Most Gracious, Most Merciful, and Master of the Day of Judgment.

translations.text: Thee do we worship, and Thine aid we seek.

verses.text: Guide us to the straight path.
verses.explanation: This opening chapter of the Quran, Al-Fatiha, is a prayer for guidance, praising God as the Most Gracious, Most Merciful, and Master of the Day of Judgment.

verses.text: The path of those upon whom You have bestowed favor, not of those who have evoked [Your] anger or of those who are astray.
verses.explanation: This opening chapter of the Quran, Al-Fatiha, is a prayer for guidance, praising God as the Most Gracious, Most Merciful, and Master of the Day of Judgment.

verses.text: Allah! There is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of existence. Neither drowsiness overtakes Him nor sleep. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth.
verses.explanation: This verse from Al-Baqarah (The Cow) reminds us that God does not burden a soul beyond what it can bear, and that truth stands clear from error with no compulsion in religion.

verses.text: There is no compulsion in religion. The right course has become distinct from the wrong.
verses.explanation: This verse from Al-Baqarah (The Cow) reminds us that God does not burden a soul beyond what it can bear, and that truth stands clear from error with no compulsion in religion.

verses.text: Allah does not charge a soul except [with that within] its capacity. It will have [the consequence of] what [good] it has gained, and it will bear [the consequence of] what [evil] it has earned.
verses.explanation: This verse from Al-Baqarah (The Cow) reminds us that God does not burden a soul beyond what it can bear, and that truth stands clear from error with no compulsion in religion.

verses.text: Every soul will taste death, and you will only be given your full compensation on the Day of Resurrection.
verses.explanation: This verse from Ali 'Imran (The Family of Imran) declares that God is the sustainer of all, knows all things, and that there is no god but Him.

verses.text: Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth and the alternation of the night and the day are signs for those of understanding.
verses.explanation: This verse from Ali 'Imran (The Family of Imran) declares that God is the sustainer of all, knows all things, and that there is no god but Him.

verses.text: Who remember Allah while standing or sitting or [lying] on their sides and give thought to the creation of the heavens and the earth, saying, 'Our Lord, You did not create this aimlessly.'
verses.explanation: This verse from Ali 'Imran (The Family of Imran) declares that God is the sustainer of all, knows all things, and that there is no god but Him.

verses.text: O mankind, fear your Lord, who created you from one soul and created from it its mate and dispersed from both of them many men and women.
verses.explanation: This verse from An-Nisa (The Women) commands justice and kindness in family and society, reminding us that God watches over all.

verses.text: O you who have believed, be persistently standing firm in justice, witnesses for Allah, even if it be against yourselves or parents and relatives.
verses.explanation: This verse from An-Nisa (The Women) commands justice and kindness in family and society, reminding us that God watches over all.

verses.text: Your Lord has decre that you worship not except Him, and to parents, good treatment. Whether one or both of them reach old age [while] with you, say not to them [so much as], 'uff,' and do not repel them but speak to them a noble word.
verses.explanation: This verse from Al-Isra (The Night Journey) reminds us that God has honored humanity and that the Quran guides to what is most upright.

verses.text: Establish prayer at the decline of the sun [from its meridian] until the darkness of the night and [also] the Quran [for] dawn. Indeed, the recitation of dawn is ever witnessed.
verses.explanation: This verse from Al-Isra (The Night Journey) reminds us that God has honored humanity and that the Quran guides to what is most upright.

verses.text: Successful indeed are the believers.
verses.explanation: This verse from Al-Mu'minun (The Believers) describes the successful believers who are humble in prayer, avoid vain talk, and give charity.

verses.text: Then did you think that We created you uselessly and that to Us you would not be returned?
verses.explanation: This verse from Al-Mu'minun (The Believers) describes the successful believers who are humble in prayer, avoid vain talk, and give charity.

verses.text: Do the people think that they will be left to say, 'We believe' and they will not be tried?
verses.explanation: This verse from Al-Ankabut (The Spider) reminds that God knows those who believe and those who are hypocrites, and that the life of this world is but a test.

verses.text: And those who strive for Us - We will surely guide them to Our ways. Indeed, Allah is with the doers of good.
verses.explanation: This verse from Al-Ankabut (The Spider) reminds that God knows those who believe and those who are hypocrites, and that the life of this world is but a test.

verses.text: O mankind, indeed We have created you from male and female and made you peoples and tribes that you may know one another. Indeed, the most noble of you in the sight of Allah is the most righteous of you.
verses.explanation: This verse from Al-Hujurat (The Rooms) declares that all humanity is created from male and female and made into nations and tribes so that people may know one another, not despise one another.

verses.text: The Most Merciful.
verses.explanation: This verse from Ar-Rahman (The Most Gracious) poses the recurring question: 'Which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?' inviting reflection on divine blessings.

verses.text: Taught the Quran.
verses.explanation: This verse from Ar-Rahman (The Most Gracious) poses the recurring question: 'Which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?' inviting reflection on divine blessings.

verses.text: Then which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.explanation: This verse from Ar-Rahman (The Most Gracious) poses the recurring question: 'Which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?' inviting reflection on divine blessings.

verses.text: Blessed is He in whose hand is dominion, and He is over all things competent.
verses.explanation: This verse from Al-Mulk (The Sovereignty) declares that blessed is He in whose hand is all dominion, and that He is over all things competent.

verses.text: [He] who created death and life to test you [as to] which of you is best in deed.
verses.explanation: This verse from Al-Mulk (The Sovereignty) declares that blessed is He in whose hand is all dominion, and that He is over all things competent.

verses.text: It is He who made the earth tame for you - so walk among its slopes and eat of His provision.
verses.explanation: This verse from Al-Mulk (The Sovereignty) declares that blessed is He in whose hand is all dominion, and that He is over all things competent.

verses.text: By the morning hours.
verses.explanation: This verse from Ad-Duha (The Morning Brightness) comforts the Prophet with the assurance that the Lord has not forsaken him and that the future will be better than the past.

verses.text: Your Lord has not taken leave of you, nor has He detested [you].
verses.explanation: This verse from Ad-Duha (The Morning Brightness) comforts the Prophet with the assurance that the Lord has not forsaken him and that the future will be better than the past.

verses.text: And the Hereafter is better for you than the present.
verses.explanation: This verse from Ad-Duha (The Morning Brightness) comforts the Prophet with the assurance that the Lord has not forsaken him and that the future will be better than the past.

verses.text: And your Lord is going to give you, and you will be satisfied.
verses.explanation: This verse from Ad-Duha (The Morning Brightness) comforts the Prophet with the assurance that the Lord has not forsaken him and that the future will be better than the past.

verses.text: For indeed, with hardship [will be] ease.
verses.explanation: This verse from Ash-Sharh (The Relief) reminds us that with every hardship comes ease, and that God expands the heart of the believer.

verses.text: Indeed, with hardship [will be] ease.
verses.explanation: This verse from Ash-Sharh (The Relief) reminds us that with every hardship comes ease, and that God expands the heart of the believer.

verses.text: So when you have finished [your duties], then stand up [for worship].
verses.explanation: This verse from Ash-Sharh (The Relief) reminds us that with every hardship comes ease, and that God expands the heart of the believer.

verses.text: And to your Lord direct [your] longing.
verses.explanation: This verse from Ash-Sharh (The Relief) reminds us that with every hardship comes ease, and that God expands the heart of the believer.

verses.text: Say, 'He is Allah, [who is] One.'
verses.explanation: This verse from Al-Ikhlas (The Sincerity) declares the absolute oneness of God: He is God, the Eternal, the Self-Sufficient, He begets not nor is He begotten.

verses.text: Allah, the Eternal Refuge.
verses.explanation: This verse from Al-Ikhlas (The Sincerity) declares the absolute oneness of God: He is God, the Eternal, the Self-Sufficient, He begets not nor is He begotten.

verses.text: He neither begets nor is born.
verses.explanation: This verse from Al-Ikhlas (The Sincerity) declares the absolute oneness of God: He is God, the Eternal, the Self-Sufficient, He begets not nor is He begotten.

verses.text: Nor is there to Him any equivalent.
verses.explanation: This verse from Al-Ikhlas (The Sincerity) declares the absolute oneness of God: He is God, the Eternal, the Self-Sufficient, He begets not nor is He begotten.

verses.text: Alif, Lam, Ra. A Book which We have revealed to you, [O Muhammad], that you might bring mankind out of darknesses into the light.
verses.explanation: This verse from Surah Ibrahim (Abraham) reflects Abraham's prayer of gratitude and the nature of God's guidance for those who believe.

verses.text: And [remember] when your Lord proclaimed, 'If you are grateful, I will surely increase you [in favor].'
verses.explanation: This verse from Surah Ibrahim (Abraham) reflects Abraham's prayer of gratitude and the nature of God's guidance for those who believe.

verses.text: When the youths retreated to the cave and said, 'Our Lord, grant us from Yourself mercy and prepare for us from our affair right guidance.'
verses.explanation: This verse from Al-Kahf (The Cave) declares that the Quran is a straight guide, warning of severe punishment while giving glad tidings to the believers.

verses.text: Wealth and children are but the adornment of the worldly life. But the enduring good deeds are better to your Lord for reward and better for [one's] hope.
verses.explanation: This verse from Al-Kahf (The Cave) declares that the Quran is a straight guide, warning of severe punishment while giving glad tidings to the believers.

verses.text: Have those who disbelieved not considered that the heavens and the earth were a joined entity, and We separated them?
verses.explanation: This verse from Al-Anbiya (The Prophets) affirms that the messengers were sent with mercy and that God's justice prevails over all.

verses.text: And We place the scales of justice for the Day of Resurrection, so no soul will be treated unjustly at all.
verses.explanation: This verse from Al-Anbiya (The Prophets) affirms that the messengers were sent with mercy and that God's justice prevails over all.

verses.text: And the servants of the Most Merciful are those who walk upon the earth easily, and when the ignorant address them [harshly], they say [words of] peace.
verses.explanation: This verse from Al-Furqan (The Criterion) praises the One who sent down the Criterion to distinguish truth from falsehood and warn all beings.

verses.text: And those who say, 'Our Lord, grant us from among our spouses and offspring comfort to our eyes.'
verses.explanation: This verse from Al-Furqan (The Criterion) praises the One who sent down the Criterion to distinguish truth from falsehood and warn all beings.

verses.text: And among His signs is that He created for you mates from among yourselves, that you may dwell in tranquility with them, and He has put love and mercy between your hearts.
verses.explanation: This verse from Ar-Rum (The Romans) affirms that God originates creation and will repeat it, and that His is the command from beginning to end.

verses.text: And We have enjoined upon man [care] for his parents. His mother carried him with hardship upon hardship.
verses.explanation: This verse from Luqman shares the wisdom of Luqman: be grateful to God, enjoin good, forbid evil, and be patient through life's trials.

verses.text: And be moderate in your pace and lower your voice. Indeed, the most disagreeable of sounds is the braying of donkeys.
verses.explanation: This verse from Luqman shares the wisdom of Luqman: be grateful to God, enjoin good, forbid evil, and be patient through life's trials.

verses.text: O you who have believed, fulfill [all] contracts. Lawful for you are the animals of grazing livestock except that which is recited to you [herein] — not taking game as [lawful] while you are in the state of ihram. Indeed, Allah ordains what He intends.
verses.explanation: This passage establishes principles of justice, obligation, and moral responsibility in Islamic law.

verses.text: O you who have believed, do not violate the rites of Allah or [the sanctity of] the sacred month or [neglect the] sacrificial animals and garlands [that mark them] or the security of the Sacred House, those who seek the bounty of their Lord and [His] approval. But when you come out of ihram, then [you may] hunt. And do not let the hatred of a people for having obstructed you from al-Masjid al-Haram lead you to transgress. And cooperate in righteousness and piety, but do not cooperate in sin and aggression. And fear Allah; indeed, Allah is severe in penalty.
verses.explanation: This passage establishes principles of justice, obligation, and moral responsibility in Islamic law.

verses.text: Prohibited to you are dead animals, blood, the flesh of swine, and that which has been dedicated to other than Allah, and [those animals] killed by strangling or by a violent blow or by a head-long fall or by the goring of horns, and those from which a wild animal has eaten, except what you [are able to] slaughter [before its death], and those which are sacrificed on stone altars, and [prohibited is] that you seek decision through divining arrows. That is grave disobedience. This day those who disbelieve have despaired of [defeating] your religion; so fear them not, but fear Me. This day I have perfected for you your religion and completed My favor upon you and have approved for you Islam as religion. But whoever is forced by severe hunger with no inclination to sin — then indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.
verses.explanation: This passage establishes principles of justice, obligation, and moral responsibility in Islamic law.

verses.text: O you who have believed, when you rise to [perform] prayer, wash your faces and your forearms to the elbows and wipe over your heads and wash your feet to the ankles. And if you are in a state of janabah, then purify yourselves. But if you are ill or on a journey or one of you comes from the place of relieving himself or you have contacted women and do not find water, then seek clean earth and wipe over your faces and hands with it. Allah does not intend to make difficulty for you, but He intends to purify you and complete His favor upon you that you may be grateful.
verses.explanation: This passage establishes principles of justice, obligation, and moral responsibility in Islamic law.

verses.text: O you who have believed, be persistently standing firm for Allah, witnesses in justice, and do not let the hatred of a people cause you not to be just. Be just; that is nearer to righteousness. And fear Allah; indeed, Allah is Acquainted with what you do.
verses.explanation: This passage establishes principles of justice, obligation, and moral responsibility in Islamic law.

verses.text: And to Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them. He creates what He wills. And Allah is over all things competent.
verses.explanation: This passage establishes principles of justice, obligation, and moral responsibility in Islamic law.

verses.text: O Messenger, announce that which has been revealed to you from your Lord, and if you do not, then you have not conveyed His message. And Allah will protect you from the people. Indeed, Allah does not guide the disbelieving people.
verses.explanation: This passage establishes principles of justice, obligation, and moral responsibility in Islamic law.

verses.text: Say, O People of the Scripture, you are not upon anything until you uphold the Torah and the Gospel and what has been revealed to you from your Lord.
verses.explanation: This passage establishes principles of justice, obligation, and moral responsibility in Islamic law.

verses.text: Indeed, those who have believed and those who were Jews and the Sabeans and the Christians and the Magians and those who associated with Allah — Allah will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection. Indeed Allah is, over all things, Witness.
verses.explanation: This passage establishes principles of justice, obligation, and moral responsibility in Islamic law.

verses.text: Say, Shall I inform you of [what is] worse than that as penalty from Allah? [It is that of] those whom Allah has cursed and with whom He became angry and made of them apes and pigs and slaves of Taghut. Those are worse in position and further astray from the sound way.
verses.explanation: This passage establishes principles of justice, obligation, and moral responsibility in Islamic law.

verses.text: Ya Sin.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: By the wise Quran,
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: Indeed you, [O Muhammad], are from among the messengers,
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: On a straight path.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: [This is] a revelation of the Exalted in Might, the Merciful,
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: That you may warn a people whose forefathers were not warned, so they are unaware.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: Already the word has come into effect upon most of them, so they do not believe.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: Indeed, We have put shackles on their necks, and they are to their chins, so they are with heads [forced] raised.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And We have put before them a barrier and behind them a barrier and covered them, so they do not see.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And it is all the same to them whether you warn them or do not warn them — they will not believe.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: You can only warn one who follows the message and fears the Most Merciful unseen. So give him good tidings of forgiveness and noble reward.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: Indeed, We give life to the dead, and We record what they have put forth and what they left behind, and all things We have enumerated in a clear register.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And present to them an example: the people of the city, when the messengers came to it.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: When We sent to them two messengers, but they denied them, so We strengthened them with a third, and they said, 'Indeed we are messengers to you.'
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: They said, 'You are not but human beings like us, and the Most Merciful has not revealed anything. You are only telling lies.'
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: They said, 'Our Lord knows that we are messengers to you,',
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And we are not responsible except for clear notification.'
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: They said, 'Indeed, we consider you a bad omen. If you do not desist, we will surely stone you, and there will surely touch you from us a painful punishment.'
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: They said, 'Your omen is with yourselves. Is it because you were reminded? Rather, you are a transgressing people.'
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And there came from the farthest end of the city a man running. He said, 'O my people, follow the messengers.'
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: Follow those who do not ask of you [any] wage, and they are [rightly] guided.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And why should I not worship He who created me and to whom you will be returned?
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: Should I take besides Him [false] deities? If the Most Merciful intends for me harm, their intercession will not avail me at all, nor can they save me.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: Indeed, I would then be in manifest error.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: Indeed, I have believed in your Lord, so listen to me.'
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: [The angels said], 'Enter Paradise.' He said, 'I wish my people could know'
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: Of how my Lord has forgiven me and placed me among the honored.'
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And We did not send down upon his people after him any soldiers from the heaven, nor would We have done so.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: It was but one shout, and immediately they were extinguished.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: How regretful for the servants. There did not come to them any messenger except that they used to ridicule him.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: Have they not considered how many generations We destroyed before them — that they to them will not return?
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And indeed, all of them will yet be brought before Us.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And a sign for them is the dead earth. We have given it life and brought forth from it grain, and from it they eat.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And We placed therein gardens of palm trees and grapevines and caused to burst forth therefrom some springs —
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: That they may eat of His fruit. And their hands have not produced it, so will they not be grateful?
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: Exalted is He who created all pairs — from what the earth grows and from themselves and from that which they do not know.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And a sign for them is the night. We strip from it the day, and at once they are in darkness.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And the sun runs on to a term appointed for it. That is the determination of the Exalted in Might, the Knowing.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And the moon — We have determined for it phases, until it returns [appearing] like the old date stalk.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: It is not for the sun to overtake the moon, nor does the night outstrip the day. They all float in an orbit.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And a sign for them is that We carried their offspring in the laden ship.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And We created for them from the likes of it that which they ride.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And if We should will, We could drown them; then no one would respond to their cry, nor would they be saved.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: Except as a mercy from Us and provision for a time.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And when it is said to them, 'Beware of what is before you and what is behind you that perhaps you may receive mercy,' they turn away.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And no sign comes to them from the signs of their Lord except that they are from it turning away.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And when it is said to them, 'Spend from that which Allah has provided for you,' those who disbelieve say to those who believe, 'Should we feed whom, if Allah had willed, He would have fed? You are not but in clear error.'
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And they say, 'When is this promise, if you should be truthful?'
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: What they await is but one blast which will seize them while they are disputing.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And they will not be able [then] to make a bequest, nor to their people will they return.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And the Horn will be blown; and at once from the graves to their Lord they will hasten.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: They will say, 'O woe to us! Who has raised us up from our sleeping place?' [The reply will be], 'This is what the Most Merciful had promised, and the messengers told the truth.'
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: It will not be but one blast, and at once they are all brought before Us.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: So today no soul will be wronged at all, and you will not be recompensed except for what you used to do.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: Indeed, the companions of Paradise, that Day, will be in amusement, occupied [with enjoyment].
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: They and their spouses — in shade, reclining on adorned couches.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: For them therein is fruit, and for them is whatever they request [or wish].
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: [And] 'Peace,' a word from a Merciful Lord.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And [they are told], 'Stand apart this Day, you criminals.'
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: Did I not enjoin upon you, O children of Adam, that you not worship Satan — [for] indeed, he is to you a clear enemy —
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And that you worship [only] Me? This is a straight path.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And he had already led astray from among you much of humanity. So did you not use reason?
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: This is Hellfire, which you were promised.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: Enter it today for what you used to deny.'
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: Today We will seal over their mouths, and their hands will speak to Us, and their feet will testify about what they used to earn.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And if We willed, We could have obliterated their eyes, and they would race to [find] the path, but how could they see?
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And if We willed, We could have deformed them in their places, and they would not be able to go forward or backward.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And whoever We grant long life, We reverse him in creation. So will they not understand?
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And We did not give him [Muhammad] knowledge of poetry, nor is it befitting for him. It is not but a reminder and a clear Quran
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: To warn whoever is alive and justify the word against the disbelievers.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: Do they not see that We have created for them from what Our hands have made, grazing livestock, and then they are their owners?
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And We have tamed them for them, so some of them they ride, and some of them they eat.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And for them therein are [other] benefits and drinks, so will they not be grateful?
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: But they have taken besides Allah [other] deities that perhaps they would be helped.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: They are not able to help them, and they [themselves] are for them soldiers in attendance.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: So let not their speech grieve you. Indeed, We know what they conceal and what they declare.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: Does not man see that We created him from a sperm-drop? Yet he is a clear adversary.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: And he presents for Us an example and forgets his [own] creation. He says, 'Who will give life to bones while they are disintegrated?'
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: Say, 'He will give them life who produced them the first time; and He is, of all creation, Knowing.'
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: [It is] He who made for you from the green tree, fire, and then from you it ignite.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: Is not He who created the heavens and the earth Able to create the likes of them? Yes, [He is], for He is the Knowing Creator.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: His command is only when He intends a thing that He says to it, 'Be,' and it is.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: So exalted is He in whose hand is the dominion of all things, and to Him you will be returned.
verses.explanation: The heart of the Quran, this surah emphasizes the reality of revelation, resurrection, and divine power.

verses.text: Ha, Meem.
verses.explanation: This surah teaches that consultation and mutual counsel are foundations of community life, while emphasizing divine mercy.

verses.text: Ayn, Seen, Qaf.
verses.explanation: This surah teaches that consultation and mutual counsel are foundations of community life, while emphasizing divine mercy.

verses.text: Thus has revealed to you, [O Muhammad], and to those before you, Allah, the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
verses.explanation: This surah teaches that consultation and mutual counsel are foundations of community life, while emphasizing divine mercy.

verses.text: To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth, and He is the Most High, the Most Great.
verses.explanation: This surah teaches that consultation and mutual counsel are foundations of community life, while emphasizing divine mercy.

verses.text: The heavens almost break from above them, and the angels exalt [Allah] with praise of their Lord and ask forgiveness for those on earth. Unquestionably, it is Allah who is the Forgiving, the Merciful.
verses.explanation: This surah teaches that consultation and mutual counsel are foundations of community life, while emphasizing divine mercy.

verses.text: And those who take as allies other than Him — Allah is [yet] Guardian over them; and you are not over them a manager.
verses.explanation: This surah teaches that consultation and mutual counsel are foundations of community life, while emphasizing divine mercy.

verses.text: And thus We have revealed to you an Arabic Quran that you may warn the Mother of Cities [Makkah] and those around it and warn of the Day of Assembly, about which there is no doubt. A party will be in Paradise and a party in the Blaze.
verses.explanation: This surah teaches that consultation and mutual counsel are foundations of community life, while emphasizing divine mercy.

verses.text: And if Allah had willed, He could have made them one community, but He admits whom He wills into His mercy. And the wrongdoers have not any protector or helper.
verses.explanation: This surah teaches that consultation and mutual counsel are foundations of community life, while emphasizing divine mercy.

verses.text: Or have they taken protectors [or allies] besides Him? For Allah — He is the Protector, and He gives life to the dead, and He is over all things competent.
verses.explanation: This surah teaches that consultation and mutual counsel are foundations of community life, while emphasizing divine mercy.

verses.text: And in anything over which you disagree — its ruling is [to be referred] to Allah. [Say], 'That is Allah, my Lord; upon Him I have relied, and to Him I turn back.'
verses.explanation: This surah teaches that consultation and mutual counsel are foundations of community life, while emphasizing divine mercy.

verses.text: [He is] Creator of the heavens and the earth. He has made for you from yourselves, mates, and among the cattle, mates; He multiplies you thereby. There is nothing like unto Him, and He is the Hearing, the Seeing.
verses.explanation: This surah teaches that consultation and mutual counsel are foundations of community life, while emphasizing divine mercy.

verses.text: To Him belong the keys of the heavens and the earth. He extends provision for whom He wills and restricts [it]. Indeed, He is, of all things, Knowing.
verses.explanation: This surah teaches that consultation and mutual counsel are foundations of community life, while emphasizing divine mercy.

verses.text: He has ordained for you of religion what He enjoined upon Noah and that which We have revealed to you, [O Muhammad], and what We enjoined upon Abraham and Moses and Jesus: to establish the religion and not be divided therein. Difficult for those who associate others with Allah is that to which you invite them. Allah chooses for Himself whom He wills and guides to Himself whoever turns back [to Him].
verses.explanation: This surah teaches that consultation and mutual counsel are foundations of community life, while emphasizing divine mercy.

verses.text: And they did not become divided until after knowledge had come to them — out of jealous animosity between themselves. And if not for a word that preceded from your Lord until an appointed term, it would have been concluded between them. And indeed, those who were granted the Scripture after them are about it in disquieting doubt.
verses.explanation: This surah teaches that consultation and mutual counsel are foundations of community life, while emphasizing divine mercy.

verses.text: So to that [religion] invite, and stand firm as you are commanded, and do not follow their inclinations but say, 'I have believed in what Allah has revealed of the Quran, and I have been commanded to do justice among you. Allah is our Lord and your Lord. For us are our deeds, and for you your deeds. There is no [need for] argument between us and you. Allah will bring us together, and to Him is the [final] destination.'
verses.explanation: This surah teaches that consultation and mutual counsel are foundations of community life, while emphasizing divine mercy.

verses.text: By the [winds] that scatter [dust].
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And those [clouds] that carry a load [of water].
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And those [ships] that sail with ease.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And those [angels] who distribute [provisions] by command,
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: Indeed, what you are promised is true.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And indeed, the recompense is to occur.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: By the heaven having pathways,
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: Indeed, you are in differing speech.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: Turned aside therefrom is he who is turned aside.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: Destroyed are the conjecturers —
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: Who are within a flood [of confusion], heedless.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: They ask, 'When is the Day of Recompense?'
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: [It is] the Day they will be tormented over the Fire.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: [And will be told], 'Taste your torment. This is that for which you were impatient.'
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: Indeed, the righteous will be among gardens and springs,
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: Taking what their Lord has given them. Indeed, they were before that doers of good.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: They used to sleep but little of the night,
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And in the hours before dawn they would ask forgiveness,
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And from their properties was [given] the right of the [needy] petitioner and the deprived.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And on the earth are signs for the certain [in faith]
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And in yourselves. Then will you not see?
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And in the heaven is your provision and whatever you are promised.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: Then by the Lord of the heaven and earth, indeed it is truth — just as [sure as] it is that you are speaking.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: Has there reached you the story of the honored guests of Abraham?
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: When they entered upon him and said, '[We greet you with] peace.' He answered, '[And upon you] peace, [you are] a people unknown.'
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: Then he went to his family and came with a fat [roasted] calf.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And placed it near them; he said, 'Will you not eat?'
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And he sensed from them apprehension. They said, 'Fear not,' and gave him good tidings of a learned boy.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And his wife approached with a cry [of alarm] and struck her face and said, '[I am] a barren old woman!'
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: They said, 'Thus has said your Lord; indeed, He is the Wise, the Knowing.'
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: [Abraham] said, 'Then what is your business [here], O messengers?'
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: They said, 'Indeed, we have been sent to a people of criminals.'
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: To send down upon them stones of clay,
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: Marked in the presence of your Lord for the excessively defiant.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And We brought out whoever was in them of believers.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And We found not within them other than a [single] house of Muslims.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And We left therein a sign for those who fear the painful punishment.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And in Moses [was a sign], when We sent him to Pharaoh with clear authority.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: But he turned away with his supporters and said, 'A magician or a madman.'
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: So We took him and his soldiers and cast them into the sea, and he was blameworthy.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And in 'Aad [was a sign], when We sent against them the barren wind.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: It left nothing of what it came upon but that it made it like disintegrated ruins.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And in Thamud [was a sign], when it was said to them, 'Enjoy yourselves for a time.'
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: But they were insolent toward the command of their Lord, so the thunderbolt seized them while they were looking on.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And they were unable to arise, nor could they defend themselves.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And [We destroyed] the people of Noah before; indeed, they were a people defiantly disobedient.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And the heaven We constructed with strength, and indeed, We are [its] expander.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And the earth We have spread out, and excellent is the preparer.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And of all things We created two pairs; perhaps you will remember.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: So flee to Allah. Indeed, I am to you from Him a clear warner.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And do not make [as equal] with Allah another deity. Indeed, I am to you from Him a clear warner.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: Thus, there did not come to those before them any messenger except that they said, 'A magician or a madman.'
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: Did they suggest it to them? Rather, they are a transgressing people.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: So turn away from them, for you are not to be blamed.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And remind, for indeed, the reminder benefits the believers.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And I did not create the jinn and mankind except to worship Me.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: I do not want from them any provision, nor do I want them to feed Me.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: Indeed, it is Allah who is the [continual] Provider, the firm Possessor of Strength.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: And indeed, for those who have wronged is a portion [of punishment] like the portion of their companions, so let them not ask Me to hasten.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: So woe to those who have disbelieved from their Day which they are promised.
verses.explanation: This surah reminds humanity of the purpose of creation — to worship and know the Divine.

verses.text: By time,
verses.explanation: This concise surah summarizes the human condition — loss except for those who believe, do good, and promote truth and patience.

verses.text: Indeed, mankind is in loss,
verses.explanation: This concise surah summarizes the human condition — loss except for those who believe, do good, and promote truth and patience.

verses.text: Except for those who have believed and done righteous deeds and advised each other to truth and advised each other to patience.
verses.explanation: This concise surah summarizes the human condition — loss except for those who believe, do good, and promote truth and patience.
